surface.name: Spacelift Quick Start through the first stack run
surface.selection_basis: Spacelift labels this page Quick Start and presents four ordered setup stages followed by Trigger your first run.

alternatives_considered.name: Spacelift Intent
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a separate AI-assisted project surface, while the documentation navigation places Quick Start as the general entry.

alternatives_considered.name: Self-Hosted Spacelift
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: It is a separate deployment model rather than the hosted Quick Start.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Spacelift account and follow the general Quick Start until the first stack run finishes.
assumed_prior_state: No Spacelift account, source-control integration, cloud integration, stack, teammate invitation, or run is assumed.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Not established from a no-account state by public official documentation.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The intended terminal is a completed first Spacelift stack run, but the required identity-provider authorization and account-creation convergence are not publicly documented.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The downstream Quick Start says to wait for the run to finish; the public path cannot reach that signal reproducibly from no account.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: Trigger your first run, then wait for the run to finish

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The Quick Start explicitly places the first run after its four setup stages, but the current free-trial page stops at peer identity-provider controls and does not document the selected provider's authorization, return, tenant naming, or dashboard state.

prerequisites.requirement: A hosted Spacelift account in the selected Europe or United States region is required before the Quick Start screens can be used.
Signup requires choosing GitHub, GitLab, Google, or Microsoft; the public page does not document any provider's complete authorization and return sequence.
The free-trial page says signing up signifies agreement to Spacelift's Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.
The downstream route requires supported source code, an infrastructure vendor, and the integrations and values selected by the developer.

primary_path.action: Open the Spacelift Quick Start and review its source-code, cloud-account, first-stack, teammate, and first-run sequence.
primary_path.output: The documented downstream route is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: The page identifies Trigger your first run after four key setup steps.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented on the Quick Start landing page.

primary_path.action: Open Start for free and choose the Europe or United States account region.
details: The page advertises an unlimited 14-day trial and says no credit card is required.
primary_path.output: GitHub, GitLab, Google, and Microsoft signup controls are shown.
primary_path.success_signal: One region is selected and the four provider choices are available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: FedRAMP setup is routed to contact the Spacelift team.

primary_path.action: Choose one of the four identity providers to begin signup.
primary_path.input: Selected identity provider and required legal agreement
primary_path.success_signal: No completed-account or dashboard state is documented on the public official surface.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provider authorization, return to Spacelift, account naming, initial role, and dashboard convergence are not enumerated.

candidate_path_gap.reason: The public free-trial page exposes region and four identity-provider choices but does not document one provider's authorization, verification, return-to-Spacelift, hosted-account creation, or dashboard transition.
candidate_path_gap.impact: The source, cloud, stack, invitation, and first-run stages cannot be connected chronologically to a no-account starting state without inference.

branches.path: Contact Spacelift for setup instead of using the public regional trial.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Introduces an external assisted-access path with no public completion timing.

branches.path: Use GitHub, GitLab, Google, or Microsoft authorization.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes the hidden authentication transition before the routes can converge on a hosted account.

branches.path: Select the matching region, enter the Spacelift account name, and select Login.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Bypasses signup but does not apply to the no-account path.

friction_gates.description: The account region must be selected before signup and later login must use the same region.
All public signup options hand control to an external identity provider whose completion and return are not documented by Spacelift.
The page ties signup to agreement with the Terms of Service and Privacy Policy.

sources.title: Get started with Spacelift | Free Trial | Spacelift
Integrate source code with Spacelift - Spacelift Documentation
Integrate cloud providers with Spacelift - Spacelift Documentation
sections_used: Set up your cloud provider integration
evidence_supported: AWS, Azure, GCP, and OIDC choices

sources.title: Create a stack in Spacelift - Spacelift Documentation
Invite teammates to your Spacelift instance - Spacelift Documentation
sections_used: Invite teammates to your Spacelift instance

uncertainties.question: What exact public sequence creates and opens a hosted Spacelift account after a new user chooses an identity provider?
uncertainties.impact: Without that bridge, none of the authenticated Quick Start steps form an end-to-end no-account path.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official trial page stops at external provider controls and the login page assumes an account name.

excluded_after_success: Fixing the starter repository's purposeful plan-policy issue occurs after the first run.
Policies, contexts, production hardening, self-hosting, migration, and ongoing run operations occur after the intended terminal.
